intro: Established in 1978, the Department of Housing Preservation and Development is the largest municipal housing preservation and development agency in the nation, charged with promoting the quality, safety, and affordability of the city's housing stock. HPD enforces the Housing Maintenance Code, finances and preserves affordable housing, and helps New Yorkers find homes they can afford, while working with communities to sustain neighborhood strength and diversity.

cardHighlights.function: Enforces housing code and finances affordable housing development
cardHighlights.scale: Performs more than 500,000 housing inspections a year
